text.title: Card Sorting Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding Card Sorting. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Run open sort first to discover natural groupings, then closed sort to validate; running only closed sort biases toward team-drafted categories.
rule.rationale: Closed-only sort cannot surface the categories the team missed.

rule.statement: Each card carries a short scope sentence; bare titles cause participants to invent context.
rule.rationale: Without context, participants sort by surface label only.

rule.statement: Minimum 15 representative participants per segment for statistical reliability of clusters.
rule.rationale: Below 15 the cluster dendrogram is noise.

rule.statement: Internal team members do not participate; they bias toward the existing taxonomy.
rule.rationale: Team sorters reproduce the team's prior mental model.

rule.statement: Synthesis uses agreement-score and similarity-matrix tools; intuitive grouping by the researcher is not enough.
rule.rationale: Eyeball synthesis loses the signal in noisy sorts.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-card-sorting.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
